assumptions.repo_review_status: no repo claims yet; use 'based on repo review' later

design_rules: drills before deeper theory in the UI
hybrid benchmark logic: starter benchmark + personal baseline
store detected issue separately from suspected cause
for mirrored left-right patterns, invert signs of face_angle, club_path, face_to_path, and launch_direction

severity_scale.high: ball-in-play problem, chunk/blade problem, or major scoring leak
severity_scale.medium: clear scoring leak or reliability issue that should be trained soon
severity_scale.low: optimization issue after bigger problems are stable

root_cause_principles: Ball flight shows impact result first, not exact body motion.
Do not diagnose grip, backswing, or release from one shot pattern alone.
For hooks and slices, check face angle, face-to-path, launch direction, and strike location before blaming one body part.
For fat and thin shots, check low point consistency and setup before adding complex swing thoughts.
For wedges, chipping, and putting, prioritize start line and distance control over advanced mechanics.

issues.beginner_description: Your driver starts right and curves more right.

secondary_checks: smash_factor_avg < 1.42 can make the pattern worse
heel strike can add fade curvature even if face_to_path is not extreme

setup: ball too far forward with poor face control
decision_process: trying to steer it into play
aiming left and leaving face open

prioritized_drills.name: Face control start-line gate
steps: Place an intermediate target 2-3 meters in front of the ball.
Hit 10 half-speed drivers and log only start direction.
Hit 10 normal-speed drivers with the goal that the ball starts inside a narrow gate.

prioritized_drills.name: Split-hand release drill
steps: Grip with trail hand 2-3 cm lower on the handle.
Make slow practice swings feeling the face close earlier.
Hit 12 shots at 70% speed and monitor face angle.

behavioral: player can call the start line before seeing the full flight
player stops chasing distance during the fix

coaching_notes: Start line matters more than total distance here.
If impact_offset is strongly heel-side, fix strike before over-correcting release.
common_pitfalls: trying to fix slice by only swinging more to the right
adding more speed before face control improves

note_prompts: Did the ball start right or just curve right?

issues.beginner_description: Your driver starts right and stays mostly right without much curve.

secondary_checks: club_path may also be slightly right
this is more a start-line problem than a curvature problem

setup: ball too far forward with passive face
decision_process: fear of left miss
over-committing to keep it in play

prioritized_drills.name: Start-line only challenge
steps: Ignore curve and distance.
Score each shot only as left of gate, in gate, or right of gate.
Do not change target for 15 balls.

behavioral: player trusts starting the ball nearer the target
player stops aiming excessively left or right to compensate

coaching_notes: A block is often simpler than a slice: fix the face relative to target first.
If face_to_path is neutral, avoid big path changes.
common_pitfalls: trying to manufacture draw without fixing the start line
changing aim every swing

note_prompts: Did the ball start right immediately?

issues.beginner_description: Your driver starts left or near target and turns too far left.

secondary_checks: toe strike can add hook curvature
launch_direction may be left or slightly right depending on path

swing_process: path too far right with face not matching target
decision_process: trying to avoid slice by over-releasing the club

prioritized_drills.name: Hold-off finish drill
steps: Hit 5 half-speed drivers holding the chest open through finish.
Hit 5 normal-speed drivers with a softer hand-roll feel.
Hit 5 target drivers and watch only start line and curve.

prioritized_drills.name: Neutral-grip checkpoint
steps: Check grip strength before every shot.
Rehearse three slow-motion swings with quieter forearms.
Then hit a short block of 8 balls.

behavioral: player can hit one straight ball and one planned small fade on demand

coaching_notes: A hook is an impact result, not automatically a grip problem.
Always check toe strike before making grip weaker.
common_pitfalls: making the grip much weaker too fast
trying to fix a toe-strike hook only with path thoughts

note_prompts: Did the shot start left or curve left late?
Did it feel like toe strike?

issues.beginner_description: You miss the driver toward the heel and the ball leaks right.

secondary_checks: face_to_path may not fully explain the right curve
ball speed loss supports strike-quality issue

setup: tee too far away from lead heel
decision_process: trying to steer away from left miss

prioritized_drills.name: Toe-side tee gate
steps: Place an empty tee just outside the toe side as a strike feedback cue.
Hit 6 slow drivers trying to miss the heel side.
Hit 6 normal drivers and check impact offset.

behavioral: player feels centered contact more often
ball speed becomes less random

coaching_notes: Do not over-diagnose path when strike location already explains much of the fade.
If balance is poor, fix setup and distance from ball first.
common_pitfalls: chasing face-to-path changes before fixing strike
standing closer without checking posture

issues.beginner_description: You hit the driver off the toe and the ball dives left.

secondary_checks: curvature may look too left for the measured face_to_path alone

setup: tee too close to body line
decision_process: trying too hard to draw it

prioritized_drills.name: Center-strike spray and freeze
steps: Use face spray or impact tape.
Hit 5 slow shots holding finish for balance.
Hit 5 normal shots only if the first 5 move closer to center.

behavioral: player recognizes toe strike immediately
finish looks balanced instead of falling toward ball

coaching_notes: Toe strike can create hook curvature even when the player thinks the face did it all.
Fix spacing before giving complicated release advice.
common_pitfalls: weakening grip when contact is the bigger issue
adding more speed before strike centers up

issues.beginner_description: The drive goes high and weak and does not carry as well as it should.

secondary_checks: spin_rate above own driver baseline supports the diagnosis
high-face strike and equipment fit can change the picture

setup: too much loft delivered by setup
ball too far back with a steep hit
decision_process: trying to help the ball up

prioritized_drills.name: Sweep-the-tee drill
steps: Tee the ball normally.
Make 5 practice swings trying to brush the tee forward of bottom.
Hit 15 drivers focusing on a shallower, more sweeping strike.

behavioral: flight looks flatter and stronger
player stops trying to lift the ball

coaching_notes: This is often a delivered-loft and contact problem, not just a backswing problem.
common_pitfalls: reducing loft by changing equipment before improving delivery
trying to hit harder into the same bad impact

note_prompts: Did the ball feel weak even on centered contact?
Was the player trying to lift it?

issues.beginner_description: You do not miss in one pattern. Some go left, some go right, and it feels random.

secondary_checks: both left_miss_rate_pct and right_miss_rate_pct above 25 support chaos rather than one bias

setup: address changes from shot to shot
decision_process: too many swing thoughts
constantly changing target or shape intention

prioritized_drills.name: Same setup, same target, same finish drill
steps: Use one target for all 15 balls.
Freeze setup for 3 seconds before each swing.
Hold finish after each shot and log only whether contact and start line were stable.

behavioral: routine looks the same each shot
player can describe one intended shot shape

coaching_notes: Do not over-prescribe body mechanics until chaos reduces.
Stability beats optimization here.
common_pitfalls: giving the player three fixes at once
changing club, target, and feel every swing

note_prompts: Was tempo the same every swing?

issues.beginner_description: Your long iron or hybrid starts right and keeps leaking right.

secondary_checks: carry often falls below club baseline because of weak strike

decision_process: trying to lift the long club instead of compressing it

prioritized_drills.name: Hybrid/long-iron start-line ladder
steps: Hit 6 half-swings trying to start the ball just left of target.
Hit 6 normal swings and log only start direction and curvature.

behavioral: player stops trying to lift the ball high

coaching_notes: Long-club fear often shows up as a face-control problem.
common_pitfalls: adding more path bias without fixing the face

note_prompts: Was the player trying to help it up?

issues.beginner_description: You hit behind the ball with the long iron or hybrid and lose most of the distance.

secondary_checks: low_point variability often rises
launch can be inconsistent and carry spread widens

decision_process: trying to scoop the ball up

prioritized_drills.name: Line-in-the-sand low-point drill
steps: Draw a line on the ground and put the ball just ahead of it.
Make swings trying to strike ground after the line.
Hit 15 balls and tag fat vs clean vs thin.

behavioral: divot or turf contact moves more target-side of the ball

coaching_notes: With long irons and hybrids, low point control is often a bigger issue than club path.
common_pitfalls: telling the player to swing harder at the same bad low point

note_prompts: Did turf happen before the ball?
Did ball position feel too far forward?

issues.beginner_description: You catch the long club too high on the ball and hit a weak low shot.

secondary_checks: smash can stay mediocre rather than catastrophically low

setup: handle too far forward at address
decision_process: trying to trap too much without enough balance

prioritized_drills.name: Brush-the-ground drill
steps: Make 5 rehearsal swings trying to brush the grass in the same place.
Hit 12 balls at 70% speed.
Log contact quality only.

behavioral: strike feels less stingy and more centered

coaching_notes: Thin is often a low-point and posture problem, not a need for more aggression.
common_pitfalls: moving ball too far forward and turning thin into fat

note_prompts: Did the strike feel low on face?

issues.beginner_description: Your mid or short iron starts left and stays left or keeps drifting left.

secondary_checks: face_to_path near zero suggests pull; more negative suggests pull-draw or hook

decision_process: aiming right and over-compensating with hands

prioritized_drills.name: Alignment stick start-line drill
steps: Set one stick on feet line and one on target line.
Hit 15 shots and score only whether they started left, center, or right.
Check setup before every ball.

behavioral: setup looks square and repeatable
player can aim and trust it

coaching_notes: Many pulls are setup-driven, especially for high handicappers.
common_pitfalls: rewriting backswing when alignment was the bigger issue

note_prompts: Did it start left right away?

issues.beginner_description: Your iron contact changes too much, so distance and direction are unreliable.

secondary_checks: impact height/offset variance supports strike inconsistency
left and right misses may both appear

decision_process: changing swing thought every ball

prioritized_drills.name: 10-ball repeatability block
steps: Use one club and one target.
Keep the exact same pre-shot routine for all 10 shots.
Score only clean strike and playable finish.

behavioral: routine looks calmer
misses become more one-sided instead of random

coaching_notes: Consistency is often a stability problem before it is a mechanics problem.
common_pitfalls: working on shape before contact is stable

note_prompts: Was the routine the same each swing?

issues.beginner_description: Your iron hits the ground too early and comes out short and heavy.

secondary_checks: low_point consistency often worsens

decision_process: trying to help the iron airborne

prioritized_drills.name: Ball-then-turf line drill
steps: Draw a line and place the ball just ahead of it.
Rehearse striking line after the ball.
Hit 15 balls and tag contact each time.

behavioral: player stops trying to scoop the ball

coaching_notes: For high handicappers, ball position and pressure shift are often the first checks.
common_pitfalls: moving ball too far back and creating low left pulls

issues.beginner_description: Your iron catches too much ball and not enough turf, and the shot comes out low or hot.

secondary_checks: carry may be inconsistent rather than always short

decision_process: trying to trap too much instead of brushing turf properly

prioritized_drills.name: Brush-and-hold drill
steps: Make rehearsal swings with a small brush of turf after the ball.
Hit 12 balls at 70-80% speed.
Hold finish and log only contact quality.

behavioral: contact sound becomes less clicky and more compressed

coaching_notes: Thin and fat are often neighbors. Fix low point without overcorrecting.
common_pitfalls: shoving the ball too far forward and turning thin into fat

note_prompts: Did strike feel low on face?

issues.beginner_description: Your wedge goes different distances too often, even when the swing feels similar.

secondary_checks: face_angle_sd and spin_loft variability can support the diagnosis

decision_process: no clear flight/landing intention

prioritized_drills.name: 3-distance ladder
steps: Choose 3 distances such as 25m, 35m, and 45m.
Hit 10 balls to each target using the same routine.
Score only balls inside the window.

behavioral: player uses one clear landing plan per shot
tempo looks more repeatable

coaching_notes: For wedge training, averages matter less than window rate and miss size.
common_pitfalls: only tracking average carry
changing backswing length and tempo together

note_prompts: Where did the ball land vs intended spot?

issues.beginner_description: You hit behind the wedge and the ball comes out short and heavy.

secondary_checks: low point variability usually supports the diagnosis

setup: too much handle drag in setup
decision_process: fear of hitting it hard enough

prioritized_drills.name: Lead-side pressure wedge drill
steps: Set 60-70% pressure on lead side at address.
Hit 15 short wedges keeping chest moving through.
Tag each shot as clean, fat, or thin.

behavioral: player keeps turning through strike instead of stalling

coaching_notes: With high handicappers, deceleration is often as important as technique.
common_pitfalls: slowing down too much in fear of blading it

issues.beginner_description: You catch the wedge too high on the ball and it shoots low and too far.

secondary_checks: impact height and spin rate can help when available

decision_process: trying not to hit it fat

prioritized_drills.name: Brush-the-grass wedge ladder
steps: Make three rehearsal swings brushing the grass.
Hit 12 wedges at one target with steady tempo.
Log launch feel and finish distance.

behavioral: strike sounds softer and more controlled
player stops stabbing at the ball

coaching_notes: Many blade fixes start with reducing fear, not adding technique words.
common_pitfalls: trying to fix blades by dramatically flipping the club

note_prompts: Was the player afraid of fat contact?

issues.beginner_description: The chip comes out short because the club hits the ground too early.

secondary_checks: low point inconsistency supports the diagnosis when measured

decision_process: trying to scoop the ball up

prioritized_drills.name: Weight-forward chip block
steps: Set 60-70% weight on lead side.
Hit 20 chips from one lie and one distance.
Score only clean contact and leave bucket.

behavioral: player turns through without scooping

coaching_notes: Keep the first fix simple: setup and low point.
common_pitfalls: adding too much wrist hinge in a panic

issues.beginner_description: The chip shoots low and too far because contact is too high on the ball.

secondary_checks: launch looks too low and speed too hot

decision_process: trying not to chunk it

prioritized_drills.name: Soft-landing brush drill
steps: Pick a landing spot close to the edge of the green.
Hit 15 chips with a soft tempo and brush-the-grass feel.
Log whether the ball landed near the spot.

behavioral: contact sounds softer and trajectory softens

coaching_notes: Fear of chunk and fear of blade often alternate. Do not fix one by causing the other.
common_pitfalls: adding too much shaft lean

note_prompts: Did the player jab at it?

issues.beginner_description: Your chip contact may be okay, but the leave distance is too random.

secondary_checks: inconsistent landing spot choice often explains the pattern

swing_process: different strike pattern shot to shot
decision_process: no clear carry-versus-roll plan

prioritized_drills.name: One club, three landing spots
steps: Use one chipping club only.
Pick three landing spots and hit 6 balls to each.
Score only whether the ball finished in the target leave bucket.

behavioral: player describes carry and roll plan before each chip

coaching_notes: Distance control is often a planning issue before it is a technique issue.
common_pitfalls: switching clubs every rep during learning

issues.beginner_description: The putt starts right of where you intended.

secondary_checks: if face data is available, putter face open at impact supports the call

decision_process: steering the putt instead of rolling it

prioritized_drills.name: Short putt start-line gate
steps: Set a tee gate 30-50 cm in front of the ball.
Hit 20 putts from 1-2 meters through the gate.
Score only gate success first, make result second.

behavioral: student commits to line and pace together

coaching_notes: Start-line skill should usually be trained before break-reading complexity.
common_pitfalls: looking up early and blaming the read

note_prompts: Was the face aimed right at address?
Did pace feel jabby or smooth?

issues.beginner_description: The putt starts left of where you intended.

secondary_checks: if face data is available, face closed at impact supports the diagnosis

swing_process: hit impulse instead of roll impulse
decision_process: trying to hold the line too tightly

prioritized_drills.name: Two-tee gate from 1.5m
steps: Create a small gate just wider than the ball.
Roll 20 putts through the gate.
Log start line before makes.

behavioral: player feels a smoother roll rather than a hit

coaching_notes: Pulls often blend aim and face-control issues. Check both.
common_pitfalls: changing read when start line was the bigger issue

note_prompts: Did it feel like a hit instead of a roll?

issues.beginner_description: Your lag putts finish too far past the hole too often.

secondary_checks: green-speed note matters a lot here

decision_process: trying to avoid being short at all costs

prioritized_drills.name: Past-hole line drill
steps: Set a stop line 50-80 cm past the hole.
Roll 15 lag putts from 8-12 meters.
Score only whether the ball finishes between hole and stop line.

behavioral: player matches pace to landing point, not fear

coaching_notes: Lag putting is usually a pace skill, not a stroke rebuild.
common_pitfalls: practicing only on one green speed

note_prompts: What green speed did it feel like?
Was the intent defensive or aggressive?

issues.beginner_description: Your lag putts die short too often.

secondary_checks: fear of long comeback putts often shows up in notes

decision_process: fear of going long

prioritized_drills.name: To-the-hole-plus drill
steps: Create a landing zone 30-60 cm past the hole.
Roll 15 lag putts trying to finish in the zone.
Do not count makes; count finish zone only.

behavioral: stroke length looks committed

coaching_notes: Short lag putts are often fear-driven, not mechanically broken.
common_pitfalls: trying to fix lag pace with only technical stroke thoughts

note_prompts: Did the player fear the comeback putt?

issues.beginner_description: Short putts are costing too many easy strokes.

secondary_checks: start-line gate misses can explain a lot of this

swing_process: jab, decel, or quit through impact
decision_process: too outcome-focused
fear of missing

prioritized_drills.name: 5-in-a-row ladder
steps: Start at 1 meter and make 5 in a row before moving back.
If one misses, restart at the same distance.
Track both make rate and start-line quality.

behavioral: routine stays the same under pressure

coaching_notes: Do not bury this inside big dashboards. This is often one of the most important scoring leaks.
common_pitfalls: working on full-stroke mechanics when short-putt routine is the bigger issue

note_prompts: Was the routine identical on every rep?
